items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
# *Indian Standard*
items.value: Indian Standard

items.md: # CODE OF SAFETY FOR THIONYL CHLORIDE
items.value: CODE OF SAFETY FOR THIONYL CHLORIDE

items.md: ## 0. FOREWORD
items.value: 0. FOREWORD

items.md: **0.1** This Indian Standard was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 13 February 1981, after the draft finalized by the Chemical Hazards Sectional Committee had been approved by the Chemical Division Council.
items.value: 0.1 This Indian Standard was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 13 February 1981, after the draft finalized by the Chemical Hazards Sectional Committee had been approved by the Chemical Division Council.

items.md: **0.2** Thionyl chloride, also known as sulphurous oxychloride is a pale yellow to red liquid with a sharp odour. Thionyl chloride is used as a catalyst and chlorinating agent in chemical industry; it is also used in organic synthesis, pesticides, engineering and plastic industries.
items.value: 0.2 Thionyl chloride, also known as sulphurous oxychloride is a pale yellow to red liquid with a sharp odour. Thionyl chloride is used as a catalyst and chlorinating agent in chemical industry; it is also used in organic synthesis, pesticides, engineering and plastic industries.

items.md: **0.3** Thionyl chloride is very toxic, and in the presence of moisture it yields toxic decomposition products, namely, hydrogen chloride and sulphur dioxide. Though no hygienic standard of permissible exposure has been published, a twenty minute exposure to 17.5 ppm has been found in experiments on cats to be fatal.
items.value: 0.3 Thionyl chloride is very toxic, and in the presence of moisture it yields toxic decomposition products, namely, hydrogen chloride and sulphur dioxide. Though no hygienic standard of permissible exposure has been published, a twenty minute exposure to 17.5 ppm has been found in experiments on cats to be fatal.

items.md: **0.4** In the preparation of this code, assistance has been derived from the following publication:
items.value: 0.4 In the preparation of this code, assistance has been derived from the following publication:

items.md: SAX ( NI ). *Dangerous Properties of Industrial Chemicals.* 1975. Fourth Edition. Van Nostrand Reinhold Co, New York.
items.value: SAX ( NI ). Dangerous Properties of Industrial Chemicals. 1975. Fourth Edition. Van Nostrand Reinhold Co, New York.

items.md: ## 1. SCOPE
items.value: 1. SCOPE

items.md: **1.1** This standard describes important properties of thionyl chloride, the nature of hazards associated with it, and essential information and directions on storage, handling, marking, labelling, preventive measures, environmental monitoring and first-aid.
items.value: 1.1 This standard describes important properties of thionyl chloride, the nature of hazards associated with it, and essential information and directions on storage, handling, marking, labelling, preventive measures, environmental monitoring and first-aid.

items.md: ## 2. TERMINOLOGY
items.value: 2. TERMINOLOGY

items.md: **2.1** For the purpose of this standard the definitions given in IS : 4155-1966\* and IS : 4167-1981† shall apply.
items.value: 2.1 For the purpose of this standard the definitions given in IS : 4155-1966* and IS : 4167-1981† shall apply.

items.md:
\*Glossary of terms relating to chemical and radiation hazards and hazardous chemicals.
†Glossary of terms relating to air pollution (*first revision*).
items.value:
*Glossary of terms relating to chemical and radiation hazards and hazardous chemicals.
†Glossary of terms relating to air pollution (first revision).

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
# 3. PROPERTIES OF THIONYL CHLORIDE
items.value: 3. PROPERTIES OF THIONYL CHLORIDE

items.md: ## 3.1 The important properties of thionyl chloride are as follows:
items.value: 3.1 The important properties of thionyl chloride are as follows:

items.md:
* a) *Chemical Name*: Thionyl chloride; sulphur oxychloride; sulphurous oxychloride
* b) *Chemical Formula*: SOCl<sub>2</sub>
* c) *Molecular Weight*: 118.97
* d) *Description*: Fuming liquid, colourless to pale yellow or yellow to red, clear liquid
* e) *Odour*: Characteristic pungent odour similar to that of sulphur dioxide; vapour irritates respiratory system, eyes and skin
* f) *Boiling Point, °C*: 78.8 at 746 mm
  * i) Technical grade: 77
  * ii) 100% pure material: 78.8 at 746 mmHg
* g) *Freezing Point, °C*: -105
* h) *Vapour Density*: 4.1 ( air = 1 )
* j) *Vapour Pressure at 27°C*: 110 mmHg
* k) *Relative Density 15.5°C/150.5°C*: 1.640
* m) *Viscosity at 0°C*: 0.01 cp
  * *Viscosity at 30°C*: 0.545 cp
* n) *Percentage in 'Saturated' Air at 27°C*: 14.5
* p) *Density of Saturated Air at 27°C*: 1.5 ( air = 1 )
* q) *Solubility*: Miscible with benzene, chloroform and carbon tetrachloride in all proportions
* r) *Chemical Reactivity*: Reacts violently with water and fumes in moist air. Hydrolyzed by water into sulphur dioxide and hydrochloric acid. Decomposes into chlorine, sulphur dioxide and sulphur monochloride when heated above 140°C
* s) *Threshold Limit Value ( TLV )*: 5 ppm\*

items.md: \*This is based on 'Toxic and Hazardous Industrial Chemicals Safety Manual', issued by the International Technical Information Institute, Tokyo, Japan.
items.value: *This is based on 'Toxic and Hazardous Industrial Chemicals Safety Manual', issued by the International Technical Information Institute, Tokyo, Japan.

items.md: <naikassociates110@gmail.com>
## 4. HAZARDS ASSOCIATED WITH THIONYL CHLORIDE
items.value: 4. HAZARDS ASSOCIATED WITH THIONYL CHLORIDE

items.md: ### 4.1 Health Hazards and Toxicity
items.value: 4.1 Health Hazards and Toxicity

items.md: #### 4.1.1 Thionyl chloride vapour irritates respiratory system, eyes and skin. The liquid is highly corrosive and causes burns on the skin, eyes and mucous membranes, whenever it comes in contact with these parts. Its ingestion results in severe internal irritation and damage. However, no permanent ill effect is reported.
items.value: 4.1.1 Thionyl chloride vapour irritates respiratory system, eyes and skin. The liquid is highly corrosive and causes burns on the skin, eyes and mucous membranes, whenever it comes in contact with these parts. Its ingestion results in severe internal irritation and damage. However, no permanent ill effect is reported.

items.md: #### 4.1.2 Symptoms — Conjunctivitis, dermatitis, rhinitis (inflammation of nose) and pneumonia.
items.value: 4.1.2 Symptoms — Conjunctivitis, dermatitis, rhinitis (inflammation of nose) and pneumonia.

items.md: ## 5. STORAGE AND HANDLING
items.value: 5. STORAGE AND HANDLING

items.md: ### 5.1 Storage — Depending on the quantity, thionyl chloride may be stored in bottles made of glass which should be provided with inner cushioning and absorbent material when packed together in wooden boxes. In the case of bulk quantities, it may be stored in glass carboys covered by closely fitting wicker or bamboo work, packed individually in a wooden slatted crate iron hamper. It may also be stored in suitable heavy duty galvanized drums provided the bungs are tightly closed. Handling in this case is less hazardous than when stored in glass carboys.
items.value: 5.1 Storage — Depending on the quantity, thionyl chloride may be stored in bottles made of glass which should be provided with inner cushioning and absorbent material when packed together in wooden boxes. In the case of bulk quantities, it may be stored in glass carboys covered by closely fitting wicker or bamboo work, packed individually in a wooden slatted crate iron hamper. It may also be stored in suitable heavy duty galvanized drums provided the bungs are tightly closed. Handling in this case is less hazardous than when stored in glass carboys.

items.md: #### 5.1.1 The packed material shall be stored in cool, dry and sufficiently ventilated rooms.
items.value: 5.1.1 The packed material shall be stored in cool, dry and sufficiently ventilated rooms.

items.md: #### 5.1.2 The material shall be stored away from water.
items.value: 5.1.2 The material shall be stored away from water.

items.md: ### 5.2 The containers shall be carefully handled while loading, transporting or unloading, as damaged containers may leak and cause dangerous concentrations of its vapour in an enclosed space.
items.value: 5.2 The containers shall be carefully handled while loading, transporting or unloading, as damaged containers may leak and cause dangerous concentrations of its vapour in an enclosed space.

items.md: #### 5.2.1 Unreacted thionyl chloride escaping from the reaction vessel shall be properly scrubbed with caustic soda solution or soda ash.
items.value: 5.2.1 Unreacted thionyl chloride escaping from the reaction vessel shall be properly scrubbed with caustic soda solution or soda ash.

items.md: ### 5.3 In case of fire fighting, water shall not be used. Only dry powder or carbon dioxide fire extinguisher should be used.
items.value: 5.3 In case of fire fighting, water shall not be used. Only dry powder or carbon dioxide fire extinguisher should be used.

items.md: ## 6. MARKING AND LABELLING
items.value: 6. MARKING AND LABELLING

items.md: ### 6.1 All containers of thionyl chloride shall bear an identifying label as depicted in IS : 1260 (Part I)-1973\*. (Thionyl chloride has been
items.value: 6.1 All containers of thionyl chloride shall bear an identifying label as depicted in IS : 1260 (Part I)-1973*. (Thionyl chloride has been

items.md: \*Pictorial markings for handling and labelling of goods: Part I Dangerous goods.
items.value: *Pictorial markings for handling and labelling of goods: Part I Dangerous goods.

items.md: **6.2** Containers of waste or other materials contaminated with thionyl chloride should be labelled as follows:
items.value: 6.2 Containers of waste or other materials contaminated with thionyl chloride should be labelled as follows:

THIONYL CHLORIDE CONTAMINATED MATERIAL
DISPOSE OFF OR DECONTAMINATE USING

items.md: # 7. PREVENTIVE MEASURES
items.value: 7. PREVENTIVE MEASURES

items.md: **7.1** Areas where thionyl chloride is handled should be well ventilated. A high standard of maintenance in the whole plant should be provided to facilitate compliance with hygienic conditions.
items.value: 7.1 Areas where thionyl chloride is handled should be well ventilated. A high standard of maintenance in the whole plant should be provided to facilitate compliance with hygienic conditions.

items.md: **7.1.1** Adequate protective safety equipment in the form of PVC gloves, PVC aprons, goggles, face shield canister gas mask (preferably, compressed air mask), etc, should be worn when handling thionyl chloride.
items.value: 7.1.1 Adequate protective safety equipment in the form of PVC gloves, PVC aprons, goggles, face shield canister gas mask (preferably, compressed air mask), etc, should be worn when handling thionyl chloride.

items.md: **7.1.2** Before opening a container, complete protective clothing, goggles and effective respirator or positive air supply hood shall be worn. Clean, preferably white, PVC gloves shall be used as rubber absorbs thionyl chloride after prolonged use.
items.value: 7.1.2 Before opening a container, complete protective clothing, goggles and effective respirator or positive air supply hood shall be worn. Clean, preferably white, PVC gloves shall be used as rubber absorbs thionyl chloride after prolonged use.

items.md: **7.2 Monitoring**
items.value: 7.2 Monitoring

items.md: **7.2.1** Potential areas of leakage shall be checked on regular basis. Environmental monitoring should be carried out at different work places regularly to detect thionyl chloride concentration. Complete and accurate records of all environmental measurements should be maintained.
items.value: 7.2.1 Potential areas of leakage shall be checked on regular basis. Environmental monitoring should be carried out at different work places regularly to detect thionyl chloride concentration. Complete and accurate records of all environmental measurements should be maintained.

items.md: **7.2.2** *Determination in the Atmosphere* — Concentrations of thionyl chloride vapour in the air shall be determined by drawing contaminated air through two scrubbers containing a measured quantity of 0.1 N silver
items.value: 7.2.2 Determination in the Atmosphere — Concentrations of thionyl chloride vapour in the air shall be determined by drawing contaminated air through two scrubbers containing a measured quantity of 0.1 N silver

items.value: *Classification of dangerous goods.

nitrate solution acidified with nitric acid. When sampling has been completed, 0.1 N sodium chloride solution equivalent to the silver nitrate used shall be added and the excess chloride back titrated with 0.1 N silver nitrate.
items.value: nitrate solution acidified with nitric acid. When sampling has been completed, 0.1 N sodium chloride solution equivalent to the silver nitrate used shall be added and the excess chloride back titrated with 0.1 N silver nitrate.

items.md: # 8. SPILLS AND WASTE DISPOSAL
items.value: 8. SPILLS AND WASTE DISPOSAL

items.md: **8.1** In case of spills and leakage the affected area shall be covered with sodium bicarbonate or an equal mixture of soda ash and slaked lime. After thorough mixing, water shall be sprayed from an atomizer with adequate precautions. This mixture should then be transferred slowly to a large container of water. It is then neutralized and drained into the sewer with sufficient water.
items.value: 8.1 In case of spills and leakage the affected area shall be covered with sodium bicarbonate or an equal mixture of soda ash and slaked lime. After thorough mixing, water shall be sprayed from an atomizer with adequate precautions. This mixture should then be transferred slowly to a large container of water. It is then neutralized and drained into the sewer with sufficient water.

items.md: **8.2** Solid waste material containing significant level of residual thionyl chloride should be transferred from the plant to special containers located in designated areas within the works boundary where it shall be suitably treated.
items.value: 8.2 Solid waste material containing significant level of residual thionyl chloride should be transferred from the plant to special containers located in designated areas within the works boundary where it shall be suitably treated.

items.md: # 9. FIRST AID
items.value: 9. FIRST AID

items.md: **9.1 Inhalation** — In case of inhalation of thionyl chloride, the affected person should be removed to a clean environment and allow to rest, keeping him warm. In severe cases oxygen should be administered using intermittent positive pressure breathing apparatus, and also immediate medical attention should be obtained.
items.value: 9.1 Inhalation — In case of inhalation of thionyl chloride, the affected person should be removed to a clean environment and allow to rest, keeping him warm. In severe cases oxygen should be administered using intermittent positive pressure breathing apparatus, and also immediate medical attention should be obtained.

items.md: **9.2 Contact with Eyes and Skin** — If thionyl chloride has got into the eyes wash them with plenty of water. Remove soaked clothing and wash affected skin with soap and water. Wash contaminated clothing with water before reusing. In case of serious burns call for medical attention immediately.
items.value: 9.2 Contact with Eyes and Skin — If thionyl chloride has got into the eyes wash them with plenty of water. Remove soaked clothing and wash affected skin with soap and water. Wash contaminated clothing with water before reusing. In case of serious burns call for medical attention immediately.

items.md: **9.3 Ingestion** — Wash out the mouth thoroughly with water and give plenty of water to drink, followed by milk of magnesia or 5 percent solution of sodium bicarbonate may be used as well as bronchodilators and decogestants. Obtain medical attention.
items.value: 9.3 Ingestion — Wash out the mouth thoroughly with water and give plenty of water to drink, followed by milk of magnesia or 5 percent solution of sodium bicarbonate may be used as well as bronchodilators and decogestants. Obtain medical attention.

# INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM OF UNITS ( SI UNITS )
items.value: INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM OF UNITS ( SI UNITS )

items.md: ## Base Units
items.value: Base Units

items.md:
| QUANTITY                  | UNIT     | SYMBOL |
| ------------------------- | -------- | ------ |
| Length                    | metre    | m      |
| Mass                      | kilogram | kg     |
| Time                      | second   | s      |
| Electric current          | ampere   | A      |
| Thermodynamic temperature | kelvin   | K      |
| Luminous intensity        | candela  | cd     |
| Amount of substance       | mole     | mol    |

parse_concerns.details: col 0 'QUANTITY' expects numeric, got 'Length' in row 1

items.md: ## Supplementary Units
items.value: Supplementary Units

items.md:
| QUANTITY    | UNIT      | SYMBOL |
| ----------- | --------- | ------ |
| Plane angle | radian    | rad    |
| Solid angle | steradian | sr     |

items.md: ## Derived Units
items.value: Derived Units

items.md:
| QUANTITY             | UNIT    | SYMBOL | DEFINITION         |
| -------------------- | ------- | ------ | ------------------ |
| Force                | newton  | N      | 1 N = 1 kg.m/s²    |
| Energy               | joule   | J      | 1 J = 1 N.m        |
| Power                | watt    | W      | 1 W = 1 J/s        |
| Flux                 | weber   | Wb     | 1 Wb = 1 V.s       |
| Flux density         | tesla   | T      | 1 T = 1 Wb/m²      |
| Frequency            | hertz   | Hz     | 1 Hz = 1 c/s (s⁻¹) |
| Electric conductance | siemens | S      | 1 S = 1 A/V        |
| Electromotive force  | volt    | V      | 1 V = 1 W/A        |
| Pressure, stress     | pascal  | Pa     | 1 Pa = 1 N/m²      |
